data_freshness.note: Scheduled 06:17 CT drop had not appeared by 11:48 UTC pre-market; dispatched Risk Data Drop Action manually (run 28515301952), completed successfully in ~80s. All 52 holdings priced, zero errors.

issues.detail: CWAN delisted June 25, 2026 after $8.4B take-private by Permira, Warburg Pincus, Francisco Partners, and Temasek at $24.55/share. Still listed in portfolios.json growth sleeve; quote is frozen at the deal price ($24.56, +0.06%), confirming no active trading. This is the 4th consecutive run flagging this.
issues.action: Remove CWAN from data/portfolios.json growth sleeve

roster_integrity.universe_note: DVN mapping (formerly CTRA, merged 2026-05-07) is now correctly reflected in portfolios.json — no longer an open issue. ETHA and IBIT (Digital-Asset-ETF sleeve) are priced in the data drop but absent from portfolios.json; the file covers 50 of the firm's 52 described holdings. universe_incomplete=true until corrected.

summary.headline: Broad AI-chip rebound accelerating: CRDO +10.7%, AMD +7.6%, MRVL +7.1%, LRCX +5.5% (resolved GREEN), TSM +4.9%. QCOM sole persisting RED (memory shortage, 4th session). New AMBER: SYK -4.8% (Russell index removal + ex-div + bearish valuation report, echoes valuation key_risk) and COIN -3.6% (crypto rout, Bitcoin sub-$60k, record ETF outflows — same theme pressuring MARA/HUT). STLD resolved GREEN — Q2 guidance actually shows sequential improvement, correcting the 06-30 margin-compression read. Hyperscaler earnings watch (MSFT 7/29) moved from active AMBER flag to calendar, 28 days out.

flags.headline: QCOM -2.13%: memory shortage persisting, continuing multi-week decline
flags.detail: Qualcomm fell again to $184.75 (-2.13% from $188.77 prior close). The global memory-chip shortage constraining handset production remains unresolved; June was QCOM's worst month in 7 years and CEO Amon has warned the shortage could persist into FY2027-28. No new catalyst today, but the decline is continuing rather than stabilizing, so this stays RED for a 4th consecutive session.
flags.thesis_link: Matches QCOM key_risk #1: 'Global memory shortage constraining handset production — Q2 FY2026 guidance cut reflects supply limitations that CEO Amon warns could persist into FY2027-28, compressing near-term revenue growth.'

flags.headline: CRDO +10.69%: sharp recovery in broad AI-chip rally — downgraded RED→AMBER
flags.detail: Credo jumped to $271.51 (+10.69%) as part of a sector-wide semiconductor rally (Philadelphia Semiconductor Index +3%+ on June 30) driven by strong AI infrastructure sentiment ahead of ASML/TSMC earnings. This is price-action recovery, not new information resolving the underlying AI-capex-cycle-dependency risk — keeping this AMBER rather than resolving to GREEN until hyperscaler capex commentary in late July confirms durability.
flags.thesis_link: Relates to CRDO key_risk #3: 'AI capex cycle dependency' — today's move is the inverse (recovery), not a materialization, of that risk.

flags.headline: MRVL +7.11%: strong bounce continuing broad AI-chip rebound — downgraded RED→AMBER
flags.detail: Marvell rose to $295.32 (+7.11%) alongside the broader semiconductor rally. No fresh company-specific news resolves the CFO insider-sale overhang or the SK Hynix HBM4 ramp-delay concern flagged in prior sessions — this is sector-wide price recovery, so AMBER (recovering) rather than GREEN (resolved) pending hyperscaler capex commentary.
flags.thesis_link: Relates to MRVL key_risk #3: 'AI capex cycle moderation' — today's move is a partial reversal of the recent selloff, not a resolution of the underlying risk.

flags.headline: SYK -4.77%: Russell Growth Index removal + ex-dividend + bearish valuation report
flags.detail: Stryker fell to $314.84 (-4.77%) driven primarily by mechanical/technical factors: removal from Russell growth indexes on June 27 triggering passive-fund outflows, and the stock trading ex-dividend ($0.88/share). A newly published bearish valuation report argues SYK is overvalued, echoing (but not confirming) the thesis's existing valuation concern. No earnings, guidance, or operational news found. Watching for follow-through since the valuation critique touches an existing key risk.
flags.thesis_link: Touches SYK key_risk #1: 'Premium valuation at 19.7x forward P/E ... limited margin of safety if growth disappoints' — a bearish valuation report is a soft echo of this risk, not a materialization (no earnings/guidance event), so held at AMBER rather than escalated to RED.

evidence.source: TipRanks / TradersUnion coverage of Russell index removal and ex-dividend move

flags.headline: COIN -3.6%: crypto-market rout — Bitcoin below $60k, record ETF outflows
flags.detail: Coinbase fell to $146.27 (-3.6%) as Bitcoin dropped below the $60,000 level amid a broad crypto selloff; crypto ETFs saw record monthly outflows of $4.06B in June and the Crypto Fear & Greed Index sits in 'Extreme Fear'. This is the same crypto-sentiment risk already driving the MARA and HUT AMBER flags — grouping under one sector theme. COIN next reports earnings 2026-08-01.
flags.thesis_link: Matches COIN key_risk #2: 'Revenue concentration in crypto trading volumes makes Coinbase a leveraged bet on crypto market sentiment' — directly materializing today.

evidence.source: StockStory / Yahoo Finance coverage of Bitcoin selloff and ETF outflows

flags.headline: KEYS +2.93%: continuing to recover from June 26 drop, not yet fully resolved
flags.detail: Keysight rose to $350.15 (+2.93%), a third consecutive improving session after the June 26 -7.64% drop. Cumulative recovery is still incomplete and 'valuation concern at an elevated multiple' (noted 2026-06-29) has not been re-tested with fresh information, so keeping AMBER rather than resolving to GREEN.

flags.headline: HUT -2.34%: crypto-sector weakness persisting alongside COIN rout
flags.detail: Hut 8 fell to $115.44 (-2.34%), continuing in the same crypto-sentiment downdraft as COIN/MARA. No new company-specific news found today beyond the broader Bitcoin-below-$60k selloff.
flags.thesis_link: Matches HUT key_risk #3: 'Bitcoin price exposure remains significant' — crypto downturn continuing.

flags.headline: NXPI +0.99%: automotive chip softness persisting, no new catalyst
flags.detail: NXP ticked up modestly to $280.95 (+0.99%), roughly flat. Automotive semiconductor demand softness flagged in prior sessions has not newly resolved; recovery continues to lag AI-exposed peers (CRDO, MRVL, AMD all up far more today).

flags.headline: AEM +0.96%: stabilizing after Q2 gold-crash flag — downgraded RED→AMBER
flags.detail: Agnico Eagle edged up to $155.24 (+0.96%). No fresh confirmation either way on gold prices was found this session (search results returned a mix of dated and current-looking figures that could not be reconciled cleanly, so no new gold price is asserted here). Given the stock has stopped falling for two sessions, downgrading from RED to AMBER while continuing to watch — the underlying Q2 realized-price risk from the 06-29 flag has not been confirmed resolved, only that the stock has stopped declining.
flags.thesis_link: Relates to AEM key_risk #1 (gold price exposure) — partial stabilization, not resolution.

flags.headline: MARA -0.86%: crypto-sector weakness persisting alongside COIN rout
flags.detail: Marathon Digital eased to $13.90 (-0.86%). Move itself is small today, but persists in the same crypto-sentiment downdraft hitting COIN/HUT/ETHA/IBIT (Bitcoin below $60k, record ETF outflows). Q1 2026 EPS miss and analyst price-target cuts from 2026-06-30 remain the operative thesis-linked concern.
flags.thesis_link: Matches MARA key_risk #1 and #3: Bitcoin-price-leveraged financial performance — crypto downturn continuing.

flags.headline: LRCX resolved GREEN: second consecutive strong session (+5.52%), WFE demand intact
flags.detail: Lam Research rose to $433.485 (+5.52%), its second straight strong session after the June 29 Micron-blowout-driven bounce (+4.88%). Cumulative recovery over two sessions is now over +10%. Wafer-fab-equipment demand data continues to look intact; resolving this flag to GREEN.

flags.headline: STLD resolved GREEN: Q2 EPS guidance ($3.51-$3.55) shows sequential and YoY improvement, contradicting prior margin-compression read
flags.detail: Steel Dynamics guided Q2 2026 EPS to $3.51-$3.55 (announced June 17), up from $2.78 in Q1 2026 and $2.01 in Q2 2025 — stronger steel-segment profitability on solid demand and wider metal margins. A small $16M asset write-down from relocating a planned aluminum recycling facility (Arizona→Mississippi) is immaterial. This contradicts the 'Q2 EPS guidance miss / margin compression' characterization from the 2026-06-30 flag; today's -2.05% move ($229.43 vs $234.20) looks like routine noise rather than a continuation of that thesis. Resolving to GREEN and correcting the record.

evidence.source: Steel Dynamics Q2 2026 guidance release (June 17, 2026) via StockTitan

flags.headline: Hyperscaler capex earnings watch moved to calendar (28 days out, outside the ~2-week AMBER trigger)
flags.detail: The late-July hyperscaler earnings window (MSFT reports 2026-07-29) is 28 days away, outside the ~2-week window at which this routine's instructions call for an active AMBER flag. Moving this out of the active flags list and into the calendar/watch section; it will return as an AMBER flag when within ~2 weeks of the print, per Step 4.

deal_watch.terms: $24.55 take-private (Permira / Warburg Pincus / Francisco Partners / Temasek)
deal_watch.note: Deal completed June 25 at $24.55/share; FIRB granted June 19; no slippage. CWAN delisted from NYSE. See roster_integrity issue above — still needs removal from portfolios.json.

levels.interaction: none — SPY at $746.65, well above this support

calendar.event: Hyperscaler Q2 earnings — capex commentary watch (MSFT reports 2026-07-29) for NVDA/MRVL/AMD/TSM/CRDO/LRCX/NXPI/QCOM AI-infrastructure exposure
